title: 1.32x increased risk of melanoma in those with low BMI
summary: 1.32x increased risk of melanoma in those with low BMI
detail: Highly significant, replicated studies, p=3.6e-12. Appears to represent a greater risk in those with low BMI, which is plausible due to the known role of FTO in obesity. This SNP is in intron 8, whereas BMI SNP's are usually in intron 1. The association was much stronger for those in the lowest quartile of BMI (1.66 per allele risk) vs those in the other 3 quartiles (1.03 per allele risk - no longer statistically significant).
